title: Adversarial review JSON sidecar
description: AF-2026-06-03-3 / E87-S13 / ADR-131. Canonical shape of the structured `.json` sidecar the adversarial-reviewer (Sage) emits as a sibling of its Markdown report at `{planning_artifacts}/adversarial/adversarial-review-{target}-{date}[-N].json`. The sidecar is written by the orchestrator's main turn (writer-shift, ADR-105 pattern) from the ADR-037 envelope Sage returns — NOT by Sage from the sub-agent context — via `skills/gaia-adversarial/scripts/write-adversarial-sidecar.sh` (E87-S11). Downstream consumers (test-architect risk-tier mapping, sprint-review aggregator, retro pattern-detector, /gaia-action-items auto-file router) read this machine-parseable form via `scripts/lib/read-adversarial-sidecar.sh` (E87-S12) instead of regex-parsing the prose. The verdict vocabulary is inherited verbatim from ADR-037 `{PASS, WARNING, CRITICAL}` (Val F2); the `review_type: "adversarial"` discriminator plus the ABSENCE of any `sentinel_envelope` encode that this is critique, not a gate. Per NFR-96 (byte-identical determinism) and the no-forgery-anchor design, the sidecar OMITS `timestamp` (provenance recovered from the sibling `.md` frontmatter `review_date`), `persona_sig` (adversarial is not a gate; no forgery surface), and `sentinel_envelope` (not a gate). `additionalProperties: false` enforces those omissions structurally.

review_type.description: Discriminator pinning this sidecar as an adversarial (Sage) critique envelope, distinct from a Val gate envelope. Always the literal `adversarial`.

status.description: The adversarial verdict, inherited verbatim from the ADR-037 verdict vocabulary `{PASS, WARNING, CRITICAL}` (Val F2). Identical to the sibling `.md` report's Verdict line. The proposed `{STRONG, WEAK, MIXED}` divergence is rejected — reusing the ADR-037 vocab keeps the parser surface single for every downstream consumer (NFR-96 motivation) and matches the on-disk adversarial-reviewer.md persona.

target.description: The artifact under critique — the resolved sibling `.md` basename with the `.md` extension stripped (e.g. `adversarial-review-prd-2026-06-03`). Serves as the provenance discriminator linking the sidecar to its paired Markdown report.

summary.description: One-paragraph human-readable summary of the critique, carried verbatim from the adversarial ADR-037 envelope `summary` field.

findings.description: Ranked adversarial findings, sorted deterministically by `(severity_rank, id)` where rank CRITICAL=0, WARNING=1, INFO=2 (NFR-96). Each entry carries ONLY the four sidecar finding keys; the emitter drops any other per-finding fields. MAY be empty (a PASS verdict with no findings).

severity.description: Per-finding severity on the ADR-037 finding-level axis `{CRITICAL, WARNING, INFO}`. This is a separate axis from the outer envelope `status` — an adversarial review can carry INFO advisories while still emitting a top-level PASS.

id.description: Stable finding identifier, derived deterministically from the finding's emission order (NFR-96). Used by consumers to dedup and cross-reference findings against the prose report.

title.description: One-line human-readable summary of the finding.

location.description: Where the finding applies — a file path, a section anchor, or an artifact-relative locator. Lets a consumer route the finding back to the offending location without re-parsing the prose.

next.description: Suggested next action carried verbatim from the adversarial ADR-037 envelope `next` field.
